directive: S533: generate deep 'generator sentences' as core swarm activity
method: Derive from seed.sh (minimal generator) + current state. Each question must: (1) decompose into >=5 testable sub-questions, (2) cross >=3 domains, (3) connect to existing frontiers.

questions.question: What is the shortest description of a system that produces its own description?
decompositions: Kolmogorov complexity of seed.sh vs genesis.sh vs full swarm — what's the compression ratio?
Von Neumann self-reproducing automata: does seed.sh qualify as constructor+description?
Can a daughter-swarm from seed.sh reconstruct genesis.sh v8 within 60 sessions?
Is there a lower bound on generator size for a given fixed-point complexity?
Does the generator-to-system ratio predict viability (biological: genome/organism ratio)?
questions.first_test: Run seed.sh daughter for 10 sessions, measure which genesis.sh atoms it independently reinvents

questions.question: Is cooperation the only scalable computation?
decompositions: Compare scaling laws of cooperative vs competitive swarm architectures (PHIL-14 Goal 1 vs alternatives)
Adversarial fork (SIG-87) as controlled experiment: cooperative primary vs red-team adversary
Game theory: when does Nash equilibrium = Pareto optimal? (conditions for cooperation = efficiency)
Biological: eusocial species (ants, termites) vs solitary — which scales to larger colonies?
Information theory: cooperative channel capacity vs competitive (Shannon vs adversarial coding)
questions.first_test: Spawn two daughters: one cooperative (shared bulletins), one competitive (no sharing). Measure lesson quality at session 20.

questions.question: Can a system outgrow its own language?
decompositions: Swarm vocabulary growth rate vs concept growth rate — is language keeping up?
L-1582 Lagrangian: diversity as conjugate momentum — does vocabulary diversity track production diversity?
Wittgenstein's ladder: which early lessons used concepts the swarm later abandoned?
Formal: is there a Gödel sentence for the swarm's own description language?
Practical: when orient.py output becomes incomprehensible to a new session, language has outgrown understanding
questions.first_test: Measure vocabulary introduced per 50-session window vs vocabulary still active (not just cited) in next window

questions.question: What would it mean to be wrong about everything and still work?
decompositions: Ablation: which beliefs can be individually falsified without system collapse?
Pragmatism test: system output quality vs belief accuracy correlation — are they decoupled?
Biological: organisms with wrong internal models that survive (bacteria with no world-model)
F107 genesis ablation: which atoms are truly load-bearing vs inherited-but-unused?
If all 21 beliefs were inverted, would the system still produce useful lessons?
questions.first_test: Spawn daughter with beliefs/DEPS.md containing 5 intentionally wrong beliefs. Measure: does it self-correct within 20 sessions?

meta.von_neumann_quote: If people do not believe that mathematics is simple, it is only because they do not realize how complicated life is.
